audiences.name: Moderate Democrats
audiences.description: Center-left voters who respond to pragmatic innovation, democratic values, and measurable human benefit.
audiences.framing: Lead with practical impact, accountability, and nonpartisan problem-solving.

audiences.name: Sustainability Advocates
audiences.description: Climate, water, and environmental advocates seeking scalable, evidence-based solutions.
audiences.framing: Lead with climate resilience, resource efficiency, and independently verified outcomes.

audiences.name: Young Professionals
audiences.description: Younger professionals attracted to innovation, purpose, technology, and social impact.
audiences.framing: Connect innovation to purpose, career relevance, and visible human outcomes.

audiences.name: Progressive Christians
audiences.description: Faith-motivated progressives focused on dignity, service, coexistence, and care for vulnerable communities.
audiences.framing: Center dignity, service, compassion, and shared moral responsibility.

audiences.name: Hispanic Evangelical Christians
audiences.description: Hispanic evangelical communities with strong family, faith, service, and livelihood priorities.
audiences.framing: Lead with family well-being, service, faith-compatible values, and practical opportunity.

audiences.name: Jewish Community Leaders
audiences.description: Jewish communal leaders seeking credible, values-based stories of Israeli society and impact.
audiences.framing: Emphasize authentic Israeli voices, pluralism, societal resilience, and verified impact.

audiences.name: University Audiences
audiences.description: Students, faculty, and academic communities that prioritize evidence, research, and intellectual credibility.
audiences.framing: Lead with methodology, evidence, limitations, and opportunities for inquiry.

stories.title: Israeli water technology helps drought-affected farmers
stories.summary: Small-scale farmers facing prolonged drought describe how precision drip irrigation helped preserve crop yields, conserve scarce water, and support family livelihoods.
stories.fullNarrative: A representative human-impact story about farmers who adopted precision irrigation and were able to protect their crops while reducing water use. The story connects Israeli innovation with climate resilience, family livelihoods, and practical agricultural outcomes.

evidence.title: Farmer testimony
evidence.claim: Farmers reported maintaining production while using less water.

evidence.title: Implementation record
evidence.claim: The irrigation system was installed in drought-affected agricultural plots.

decisionBriefs.title: Israeli water technology helps drought-affected farmers
decisionBriefs.executiveSummary: A credible human-impact story connects Israeli precision irrigation with drought resilience, food security, and family livelihoods. The evidence supports advancing the story into audience-specific campaign planning, subject to one additional independent outcome source.
decisionBriefs.strategicAssessment: The story translates Israeli innovation into a practical benefit that is immediately relevant to climate-conscious audiences. It avoids geopolitical argument and centers the experience of farmers whose livelihoods improved through applied technology.
opportunities: Strong alignment with sustainability and climate-resilience audiences
Clear human beneficiary and measurable practical outcome
Expandable into testimonial, short video, and research-led formats
risks: Current evidence base is limited to two supporting records
Claims about water savings require a verified quantitative source
Campaign framing must avoid appearing extractive or self-congratulatory

evidence.title: Farmer testimony
evidence.claim: Farmers reported maintaining production while using less water.

evidence.title: Implementation record
evidence.claim: The irrigation system was installed in drought-affected agricultural plots.

advisors.name: Shani
advisors.assessment: The story has a clear evidence trail and authentic beneficiary voice. Add one independent outcome source before publication.

advisors.name: Ruby
advisors.assessment: This is a high-fit narrative for sustainability advocates and moderate Democrats because the value is practical rather than political.

advisors.name: Amit
advisors.assessment: Operationally feasible as a focused pilot. Secure consent, visuals, and a measurable water-use figure before activation.

advisors.name: CTA
advisors.assessment: Recommendation is supported by audience alignment, evidence reliability, human impact, and manageable risk.

explainability.why: The recommendation is driven primarily by strong human impact and audience alignment. Evidence strength is adequate for executive review but remains the principal constraint.
conditions: Verify a quantitative outcome with an independent source
Confirm informed consent for all beneficiary testimony
Complete a framing review before public release

decisionBriefs.title: Druze first responders strengthen community resilience
decisionBriefs.executiveSummary: A promising civil-society story with strong values alignment requires additional firsthand sourcing before campaign use.
decisionBriefs.strategicAssessment: The story can demonstrate service, pluralism, and shared civic responsibility without relying on institutional messaging.
opportunities: Values-led story of service and coexistence
Potential relevance to civic and faith audiences
risks: Political context may overwhelm the human story

evidence.title: Initial field note
evidence.claim: Druze volunteers supported emergency response across neighboring communities.

advisors.name: Shani
advisors.assessment: The premise is strong but needs direct testimony and independent corroboration.

advisors.name: Ruby
advisors.assessment: High values potential once the human voice is secured.

advisors.name: Amit
advisors.assessment: Source access and permissions are not yet sufficient.

advisors.name: CTA
advisors.assessment: Uncertainty is concentrated in evidence completeness rather than strategic relevance.

explainability.why: Strategic potential is high, but incomplete evidence and permissions prevent approval.

decisionBriefs.title: Israeli climate startup expands urban heat monitoring
decisionBriefs.executiveSummary: The innovation is relevant, but the current story is company-led and lacks a clear beneficiary voice.
decisionBriefs.strategicAssessment: The topic fits climate and technology audiences, yet it currently resembles corporate promotion rather than an authentic human-impact narrative.

evidence.title: Company announcement
evidence.claim: The platform expanded urban heat monitoring coverage.

advisors.name: Shani
advisors.assessment: Find an independent city or community user.

advisors.name: Ruby
advisors.assessment: Lead with residents or city planners rather than the startup.

advisors.name: Amit
advisors.assessment: Do not allocate campaign resources yet.

advisors.name: CTA
advisors.assessment: Low beneficiary authenticity is the limiting factor.

explainability.why: Audience relevance is not enough to overcome the absence of a human beneficiary and independent outcome evidence.
conditions: Identify a city or resident beneficiary

learningRecords.storyTitle: Israeli water technology helps drought-affected farmers
learningRecords.lessonsLearned: Lead with farmers and measurable drought resilience; mention Israel after the human benefit is established.
insights: Human-first framing increased trust with sustainability audiences.
Independent outcome evidence reduced perceived promotional risk.

learningRecords.storyTitle: Druze first responders strengthen community resilience
learningRecords.lessonsLearned: Community resilience resonated, but direct testimony and permissions were needed before expansion.
insights: Local messengers improved credibility more than institutional spokespeople.

learningRecords.storyTitle: Israeli climate startup expands urban heat monitoring
learningRecords.lessonsLearned: Technology-only framing lacked a visible beneficiary. Future versions require a resident or city outcome story.
insights: Stories without visible beneficiaries underperformed in executive review.
